query: CanSat telemetry testing guide LoRa ground station

results.title: GitHub - Bammer187/Cansat: A real-time telemetry system for CanSat missions using LoRa, Python (Flask), and Vue.js. Collects, transmits, and visualizes environmental and motion sensor data via a responsive web dashboard. Includes simulation mode for development without hardware.
results.description: This CanSat project integrates a real-time telemetry system using LoRa for long-range data transmission, Python for backend data processing and Vue.js for a responsive web dashboard. The system collects and visualizes environmental and positional data from the CanSat during flight, providing reliable communication and an intuitive interface for monitoring mission parameters. You are also able to test the GUI and Python Code without sensors.
results.content:
This CanSat project integrates a real-time telemetry system using LoRa for long-range data transmission, Python for backend data processing and Vue.js for a responsive web dashboard. The system collects and visualizes environmental and positional data from the CanSat during flight, providing reliable communication and an intuitive interface for monitoring mission parameters. You are also able to test the GUI and Python Code without sensors.
The next steps will assume that you are starting in the Cansat/ directory. ... cd Groundstation\ Raspberry/ # On Windows (PS): cd '.\Groundstation Raspberry\' python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate pip install -r requirements.txt ... Copy the dist/ folder into Groundstation Raspberry/. ... Access the UI at http://localhost:5000. ... If you are using the same sensors as we are and they are running with the LoRa modules, you can start the entire communication in this way:
After each measurement cycle, the data is transmitted via LoRa modules. The ground module is connected to a Raspberry Pi through UART. The Pi reads the incoming values, stores them in a database, and forwards them to a server.
If you encounter errors, try running the script outside the virtual environment, as this has resolved similar issues in our tests.

Utilizing license-free RF bands, LoRa technology facilitates efficient data transmission. Telemetry data is processed and displayed in real-time on a custom webpage, providing instant access to key environmental parameters like temperature, pressure, humidity, altitude, and more.

results.title: Design of CANSAT for Air Quality Monitoring at an Altitude of 900m
results.description: Following this, the telemetry data from the CANSAT was collected at the Ground Station with the help of Xbee communication module. Simultaneously,The results of the experiment were observed at the GUI Dashboard. The descent of CANSAT was timed to be approximately 25 minutes.
results.content:
Following this, the telemetry data from the CANSAT was collected at the Ground Station with the help of Xbee communication module. Simultaneously,The results of the experiment were observed at the GUI Dashboard. The descent of CANSAT was timed to be approximately 25 minutes.
The telemetry data from the CANSAT was collected at the Ground Station with the help of LoRa communication module.[10]The goal of the CANSAT project is to create a nanosatellite for the purpose of gathering telemetry data on air pollutants in Veracruz-Boca del Rio, specifically carbon monoxide, methane, benzene, and butane.
Following this, the telemetry data from the CANSAT was collected at the Ground Station with the help of Xbee communication module. Simultaneously,The results of the experiment were observed at the GUI Dashboard. The descent of CANSAT was timed to be approximately 25 minutes. The Snapshot of experiments at Ahmedabad,Gujarat.The Table 4 shows the results after the experiments. ... Thus a Novel CANSAT was designed for 727gms tested and operated at an altitude of 900 m from the ground level and intended actions such as power on of the Satellite after deployment from the satellite, Antenna Deployment, Sensor information of Air quality Monitoring and Temperature with latitude and longitude of a decent path were tested, deployed and verified.
The ground station is equipped with an RF receiving antenna and a computer interface for real-time data processing and storage. [11]The paper outlines the design and development of a CANSAT for environmental monitoring and scientific exploration, focusing on sensors, telemetry, and descent systems.
The telemetry data is also transmitted in real-time to the ground station using the MQTT protocol. An unique aerodynamic rotor mechanism with a 3 axis mechanical gyroscopic system is placed in the CANSAT to control the stabilisation of the satellite. A two-stage parachute system used for safe descent.

results.title: Telemetry system - The CanSat book
results.description: This signal is received by the ground station and sent to a laptop, where it is stored before being processed as experimental data. Students follow their descending CanSat while they receive radio signals from their CanSat via an antenna. Photo: Andøya Space · The RFM96 is a radio for 433 MHz ISM band, which is free to use and perfect for CanSat use. The radio uses a proprietary protocol called LoRa (short for long range), patented by Semtech, that is perfect for low data rate, but long range and relatively low transmission power (+20 dBm, 100 mW maximum).
results.content:
This signal is received by the ground station and sent to a laptop, where it is stored before being processed as experimental data. Students follow their descending CanSat while they receive radio signals from their CanSat via an antenna. Photo: Andøya Space · The RFM96 is a radio for 433 MHz ISM band, which is free to use and perfect for CanSat use. The radio uses a proprietary protocol called LoRa (short for long range), patented by Semtech, that is perfect for low data rate, but long range and relatively low transmission power (+20 dBm, 100 mW maximum).
This information is called the “mission data” or “scientific data”. In your CanSat this would be the information from the sensor board. This data is sent to a ground station to be studied by scientists. CanSat telemetry operations can be broken down into three distinct components: transmitting data, receiving data, and processing data.
When working as a ground station, the Teensy 4.1 will be connected and powered via an USB cable to your pc, and needs no voltage regulator or other components on the board, except the radio and sma connector. The CanSat shield board is now ready to be tested.
Andøya Space has used this radio in other activities than CanSats also, and actually we routinely use this radio in weather balloons which go up to 25 km altitude with a total distance between the ground station and the transmitter of 230 km!

results.title: Building Your Own CanSat: A DIY Guide to Miniature Space Exploration – Frank's World of Data Science & AI
results.description: One of the significant hurdles was ensuring our miniature satellite could communicate effectively with a ground station over considerable distances. The answer was found in the REYAX LoRa module, renowned for its ability to send data over kilometers using minimal power — perfect for our needs. Before finalizing the design, extensive testing on a breadboard was necessary to confirm all components worked seamlessly. Once satisfied, I transitioned the circuit to a zero PCB, soldering everything securely for permanence. Powering the CanSat independently, I added a 18650 battery in its holder, eliminating the need for USB dependency.
results.content:
One of the significant hurdles was ensuring our miniature satellite could communicate effectively with a ground station over considerable distances. The answer was found in the REYAX LoRa module, renowned for its ability to send data over kilometers using minimal power — perfect for our needs. Before finalizing the design, extensive testing on a breadboard was necessary to confirm all components worked seamlessly. Once satisfied, I transitioned the circuit to a zero PCB, soldering everything securely for permanence. Powering the CanSat independently, I added a 18650 battery in its holder, eliminating the need for USB dependency.
Have you ever wondered how you could bring your curiosity for space exploration down to Earth? Welcome to the world of CanSat, a remarkable project where technology enthusiasts and students dive in…
The most exhilarating moment was testing the CanSat’s communication and descent capabilities. During a ground range test, the satellite continued transmitting data seamlessly, affirming its robust communication link. Finally, testing the parachute from a height stimulated the same thrilling experience akin to a real satellite deployment.
Designed to fit within a regular soft drink can, a CanSat allows you to create a fully functional satellite capable of collecting real-time atmospheric data such as temperature, pressure, and humidity. What’s more? It safely descends back to the ground using a parachute.

results.title: CanSat Kit and TSL Mini Ground Station – Twiggs Space Lab
results.description: Students solder all of the components on each board. The CSK includes: all the boards, parts, and electronic components required to build a CanSat; a detailed User’s Manual for assembly, testing and operations; and software libraries.
results.content:
Students solder all of the components on each board. The CSK includes: all the boards, parts, and electronic components required to build a CanSat; a detailed User’s Manual for assembly, testing and operations; and software libraries.
The CanSat Kit (“CSK”) is an exciting and challenging project-based educational engineering system for students to acquire hands-on training in the life cycle development of a basic satellite project at an affordable price. Critical skills learned, include mission planning and design, building and testing satellite subsystems, systems engineering, project management, remote sensing and data analytics, and many other competences required for the development and management of new technologies.
Description of TSL Mini Ground Station: The TSL Mini Ground Station ("TSL MiniGS") is a basic ground station designed to support communications with HC-05 Bluetooth (included), LoRa RF98 and LoRa E5 modules and includes its own microcontroller.

results.title: CanSat - A Simulation of A Real Satellite - Sponsor - PCBWay
results.description: We decided on the following: · ... (also helpful for targeted landing) – FONA 808 · All the data will be transmitted using a LoRa (Long Range) RFM95W Transceiver....
results.content:
We decided on the following: · ... (also helpful for targeted landing) – FONA 808 · All the data will be transmitted using a LoRa (Long Range) RFM95W Transceiver....
They are responsible for all aspects: those sensors and sending it to the base station, situated on the ground. My team must build a CanSat and program it to accomplish a primary compulsory mission and a secondary one. The primary mission consists of measuring the following parameters and transmitting the following data as telemetry to the ground station at least one every second:
The secondary mission of the CanSat must be selected by the team. We decided on the following: · Targeted landing using a parafoil – using Servo Motors for steering · · Measure photosynthetic activity with a special camera filter – PTC06 Serial Camera + Infragram Filter · · Gather GPS data (also helpful for targeted landing) – FONA 808 · All the data will be transmitted using a LoRa (Long Range) RFM95W Transceiver.
The CanSat is then launched by a rocket to an altitude of about one kilometer, or dropped from a platform or captive balloon. Then its mission begins.

The CanSat is then launched by a rocket to an altitude of about one kilometer, or dropped from a platform or captive balloon. Then its mission begins.

results.title: Cansat Competition Guide 2024 Planetary Probe Instrument Delivery Mission
results.description: The second test will verify communications with the Cansat and demonstrate the ground · station software. The ground station software operations will be scored at this time.
extra_snippets: Cansat must be completed, built and ready to launch at the flight readiness review. A ball of · wires and boards does not constitute flight ready. To emphasize, when presented at the Flight · Readiness Review (FRR), it must be in a state where it can be turned on and placed into a · rocket for immediate launch and be fully operational. Flight Readiness Review will be ... Teams must be prepared to demonstrate the ground station. The first test at the FRR will be the drop test.
The second test will verify communications with the Cansat and demonstrate the ground · station software. The ground station software operations will be scored at this time.

Verify the Cansat did not lose power. ... Inspect for any damage, or detached parts. ... Verify telemetry is still being received. 2. Thermal Test - This test is to verify the Cansat can operate in a hot environment.

Power down Cansat. 4. Vacuum Test - This test is designed to verify deployment operation of the payload(s). A · vacuum chamber can be simply constructed using a bucket or pail of 5 gallons or 18+ litres. A lid can be used or a ¼ inch or 6 mm thick sheet of polycarbonate can be · placed on top of the bucket. Do not use acrylic as that can shatter. A vacuum cleaner ... Turn on the vacuum to start pulling a vacuum. ... Make the saved telemetry available for the judges to review.
